sections.html: NEXUS Security serves Texas businesses across multiple industries. Each client has unique challenges, regulatory requirements, and stakeholders. You’ll work with all four over the course of the semester.

sections.label: Brazos Financial Group — Modules 1 & 4
paragraphs: Wealth management firm managing $2.8B in assets. A recent merger brought legacy systems and compliance concerns. An SEC examination is scheduled in 6 months. Leadership knows they have problems but needs data to justify remediation budget.
Environment: Windows Active Directory   •   Key Issue: SEC compliance

sections.label: Gulf Coast Healthcare Partners — Modules 2 & 3
paragraphs: Multi-location healthcare network with 12 clinics across Greater Houston. The patient portal has certificate issues causing browser security warnings. HIPAA compliance is non-negotiable — patient trust is everything.
Environment: Mixed (Linux servers)   •   Key Issue: HIPAA compliance, patient trust

sections.label: LoneStar DevOps — Modules 3 & 5
paragraphs: Fast-growing DevOps startup seeking enterprise clients. Series B funding depends on passing enterprise security reviews. A “move fast, break things” culture is colliding with enterprise compliance requirements.
Environment: Cloud-native, containers   •   Key Issue: Enterprise security review

sections.label: Alamo Industries — Module 4
paragraphs: Third-generation family manufacturing company with DoD contracts. CMMC Level 2 certification is required to keep federal business. Legacy OT systems haven’t been updated “since the Reagan administration.”
Environment: Mixed IT/OT, Linux   •   Key Issue: CMMC certification

sections.html: You’ll learn more about each client as their modules begin. Study your stakeholders — knowing who reads your report shapes how you write it.
